title: Google adds mandatory AI-content labels and a “How this ad was made” panel
summary: Google is expanding ad transparency: a new “How this ad was made” panel in My Ad Center (Search, YouTube, Discover) shows viewers when generative AI was used to create or alter an ad, and advertisers must now use Google’s tools to label AI-generated ad content. It’s an advertiser-facing disclosure requirement, not just a viewer feature.

title: Video campaign groups: cross-campaign reach & frequency optimization now global
summary: YouTube reach-and-frequency optimization for video campaign groups is now available globally in Google Ads, letting advertisers coordinate reach and frequency across multiple video campaigns to hit optimal frequency targets. Google cites a Meridian MMM study showing an optimal ~2.7 impressions/week drives a 19% ROI lift; the capability is coming to Display & Video 360 soon.

title: Local Services Ads move into Google Ads as a Performance Max (pay-per-lead) campaign
summary: Google is migrating Local Services Ads out of the standalone LSA dashboard and into the Google Ads UI as a new “Performance Max for pay-per-lead” campaign for local service businesses, with automatic Google Business Profile syncing and unified campaign management. Rolling out in phases.

title: Google drops the $50K spend requirement for Lead Form assets
summary: Google quietly removed the long-standing $50,000 lifetime ad-spend eligibility threshold for using Lead Form assets, opening its native lead-generation format to far smaller advertisers, alongside new integrations and simplified eligibility.

title: AI Max spotted rolling into Standard Shopping campaigns
summary: AI Max—Google’s bundle of AI-powered query matching, dynamic ad-copy customization, and landing-page optimization—is appearing in Standard Shopping campaigns, a campaign type that has historically given advertisers more manual control. It suggests broader AI-Max expansion beyond Search.

title: Google Ads API v25 adds YouTube metrics, loyalty campaign goals, and new objective controls
summary: Google released Google Ads API v25, adding expanded YouTube measurement, retention/loyalty-focused campaign goals, new reporting capabilities, and new ways to manage objectives. The release includes changes that require developers to upgrade.

title: Google Ads adds “Missed Growth” estimates to the Recommendations tab (beta)
summary: A new beta surfaces “Missed Growth Opportunity” insights directly in the main Recommendations tab, estimating the clicks, conversions, and revenue an account may be losing due to limited budgets or low bids—making budget/bid constraints more visible in-account.

title: Google Ads adds bulk linking of multiple accounts to one GA4 property
summary: Google is rolling out bulk linking so advertisers can connect multiple Google Ads accounts to a single GA4 property from one place, simplifying data-sharing setup—useful for agencies and multi-account advertisers.

title: Google Tag Manager adds Guided Setup for Google Ads purchase conversion tracking
summary: A new “Guided Setup” card in Google Tag Manager walks advertisers through configuring Google Ads purchase conversion tracking, automatically setting up key measurement components and reducing the manual tag work required.

title: Google confirms DSA sunset timeline: create until Jan 2027, auto-migrate to AI Max from Feb 2027
summary: Google is sunsetting standalone Dynamic Search Ads (DSA) campaigns in favor of AI Max. After advertiser feedback, the timeline was revised: advertisers can keep creating DSA campaigns until January 2027, with automatic migration to AI Max now beginning February 2027 (previously slated for September).

title: Meta launches Business Agent, autonomous AI agents for messaging-based sales and service
summary: At Meta Conversations 2026, Meta announced the Meta Business Agent—enterprise-grade, autonomous AI agents (beyond rule-based chatbots) that handle multi-turn conversations, qualify leads, answer support, and check inventory via API while maintaining brand voice. Relevant to advertisers running click-to-message / lead campaigns; ads-manager surface still limited.
